steps.instruction: A ray starts at one point and extends forever in one direction. It has one endpoint.
steps.work: Ray = endpoint + infinite extension in one direction.
steps.explanation: Unlike a line (infinite both ways) or a segment (two endpoints), a ray has exactly one endpoint.

steps.instruction: A geometric figure starts at point A, passes through point B, and continues forever past B.
steps.work: Starts at A → passes through B → keeps going.
steps.explanation: The figure has a fixed starting point (A) and extends infinitely in one direction — that matches a ray.

steps.instruction: The endpoint is the point where the ray begins.
steps.explanation: Point A is where the ray starts, so A is the endpoint.

steps.instruction: Name a ray using its endpoint first, then any other point on it. Write a small arrow pointing right above the two letters.
steps.work: This ray is named AB (with a right-pointing arrow above AB).
steps.explanation: The endpoint always comes first when naming a ray. The second letter shows which direction it extends.

title: Explaining the Difference Between a Line and a Line Segment

steps.instruction: A line extends infinitely in both directions. It has no endpoints.
steps.explanation: The arrows show it goes on forever in both directions. We name it with any two points on it.

steps.instruction: A line segment is a piece of a line with two endpoints. It has a definite length.
steps.work: Segment: · ————— ·  (dots on both ends, no arrows).
steps.explanation: The dots represent endpoints. The segment stops at both ends, so we can measure its length.

steps.instruction: List the key differences between a line and a line segment.
steps.work: Line: 0 endpoints, infinite length, arrows on both ends. Segment: 2 endpoints, finite length, dots on both ends.
steps.explanation: The number of endpoints is the main difference. A line never stops; a segment always stops at both ends.

steps.instruction: Figure 1 has points P and Q with arrows on both sides. Figure 2 has points R and S with dots on both ends. Classify each.
steps.work: Figure 1 = line PQ. Figure 2 = line segment RS.
steps.explanation: Arrows mean the figure extends forever (line). Dots with no arrows mean it stops at both ends (segment).
